text.title: Django Services: Decision Tree, Function vs Class, Lazy Imports
text.summary: Rules for deciding when to create a service function, function-first vs class design, lazy model imports, and keyword-only argument convention.

section.title: Placement Decision Tree

rule.statement: Place logic in services/ when it changes the DB, makes outbound calls, or needs to be called from views AND Celery tasks. Place pure functions in utils/.
rule.rationale: Views calling queryset methods directly couple business logic to HTTP context and make it impossible to reuse from management commands or async tasks without importing Django's request machinery.

section.p: Decision: Changes DB (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE)? → services/. Makes outbound API calls? → services/ or integrations/. Pure function (validation, calculation)? → utils/. Data transformation? → utils/.

rule.statement: Default to service functions; use a class only when dependency injection is required (e.g., a swappable payment gateway).
rule.rationale: Classes with no DI are fake-OO: they have no state beyond what a function argument could carry. Function-first keeps the call site simple and avoids instantiation ceremony.

example.code:
# Function-style (preferred) def activate_user_item(user: "User", item_code: str, *, activated_by: "Admin") -> "Item": """Activate item for user.""" from apps.inventory.models import Item item = Item.objects.get(code=item_code) item.user = user item.is_active = True item.save(update_fields=["user", "is_active", "updated_at"]) return item
# Class-style only when DI is needed class OrderProcessingService: def __init__(self, payment_gateway: PaymentGateway): self.payment_gateway = payment_gateway
def process(self, order: "Order") -> ProcessingResult: ...

rule.statement: Import Django models inside the function body or under TYPE_CHECKING to prevent app-loading order errors in large codebases.
rule.rationale: Django apps load in a defined order. A service module that imports models at top-level triggers an AppRegistryNotReady error if it's imported before its app is ready.

example.code:
from __future__ import annotations from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING: from apps.inventory.models import Item from apps.users.models import User
def activate_item(user: "User", code: str) -> "Item": from apps.inventory.models import Item # lazy import ...

rule.statement: Mark all optional parameters as keyword-only with * in the signature to prevent positional-arg drift across versions.
rule.rationale: Positional optional parameters can be silently passed in the wrong order by callers; * enforces named call sites that break loudly when signatures change.

example.code: def create_order( user: "User", amount: "Decimal", order_type: str, *, item: "Item | None" = None, notify: bool = True, ) -> "Order": ...
